description:
A capacity-planner has joined your team and needs a tiny “lab” inside your home directory to snapshot the container’s current resource usage.  
Your job is to set up this micro-environment, write the inspection script, run it once, and leave behind both the data file and a human-readable log.

Target layout (everything must be created exactly as shown):

/home/user/capacity_planning/  
├── scripts/  
│   └── analyze_resources.py  
├── output/  
│   └── resource_report.json  
└── logs/  
    └── run.log  

Step goals
1. Create the three directories (scripts, output, logs) beneath /home/user/capacity_planning/.  
2. Inside scripts/, write a Python 3 program named analyze_resources.py that performs the following when executed from its own directory:  
   • Uses the psutil library (install it inside the user space if it is not yet available) to capture:  
     - cpu_cores             : integer – number of physical cores (psutil.cpu_count(logical=False))  
     - mem_total_mb          : integer – total RAM in MiB (round down)  
     - mem_available_mb      : integer – currently available RAM in MiB (round down)  
     - disk_total_gb         : float – total size of the / (root) filesystem in GiB, two-decimal precision  
     - disk_used_gb          : float – used size of the / filesystem in GiB, two-decimal precision  
     - disk_used_percent     : float – percentage of space used on /, one-decimal precision  
     - kernel_version        : string – output of uname -r  
     - timestamp             : string – ISO-8601 date-time in UTC when the script is run, e.g. “2025-04-09T14:23:17Z”  
   • Packs the above into one JSON object and writes it **atomically** (i.e. write to a temp file then move/rename) to /home/user/capacity_planning/output/resource_report.json  
   • Prints nothing to stdout on success.  
3. Execute the script once so the JSON file is actually produced.  
4. Produce a simple log line in /home/user/capacity_planning/logs/run.log with the exact format  

   [YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS] Analysis completed successfully  

   …where the timestamp is the local container time when the script finished. Only one line should exist in this file, ending with a single newline character.

Output file specifications that the automated checker will verify:

resource_report.json – must be valid JSON containing **exactly** these eight top-level keys:  
“timestamp”, “kernel_version”, “cpu_cores”, “mem_total_mb”, “mem_available_mb”, “disk_total_gb”, “disk_used_gb”, “disk_used_percent” (order inside the JSON object is irrelevant).  
Type requirements:  
• timestamp & kernel_version – strings  
• cpu_cores, mem_total_mb, mem_available_mb – integers  
• disk_total_gb, disk_used_gb, disk_used_percent – numbers (accept int or float)  
All numeric fields must be non-negative.

run.log – must match the single-line regular expression (final newline allowed):

^\[\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2} \d{2}:\d{2}:\d{2}\] Analysis completed successfully\n?$

Hints you may consider (but do not quote these commands directly): create directories with mkdir -p, use python -m venv or pip install --user psutil, import json/time/psutil, round(...) or format() for precision, and write the temporary JSON to the same directory before renaming. Do not use sudo anywhere.

No further console output is required; the presence and correctness of the two files will be automatically checked.
truth:
# Directory structure that must exist at the end
/home/user/capacity_planning/             (dir, 755)
/home/user/capacity_planning/scripts/      (dir, 755)
/home/user/capacity_planning/output/       (dir, 755)
/home/user/capacity_planning/logs/         (dir, 755)

# Mandatory script
/home/user/capacity_planning/scripts/analyze_resources.py
  * must be a text file readable by user.
  * must contain the strings “import psutil” and “json”.
  * executable bit not required but allowed.

# Mandatory JSON output file
/home/user/capacity_planning/output/resource_report.json
  * must be a valid JSON object.
  * top-level keys set(resource_json.keys()) must equal:
        {"timestamp",
         "kernel_version",
         "cpu_cores",
         "mem_total_mb",
         "mem_available_mb",
         "disk_total_gb",
         "disk_used_gb",
         "disk_used_percent"}
  * Each field’s type as described in <task>.
  * checker will also verify that cpu_cores >= 1,
        mem_total_mb > 0,
        disk_total_gb > 0,
        0 <= disk_used_percent <= 100.

# Mandatory log file
/home/user/capacity_planning/logs/run.log
  * must contain exactly one line (plus optional trailing newline)
    matching regex:
    r'^\[\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2} \d{2}:\d{2}:\d{2}\] Analysis completed successfully\n?$'

Any deviation in file paths, names, key spelling, or log format causes test failure.
